baseline_card.reference_linear_truth_anchor: `reference_linear` remains the measured baseline truth anchor for the current `trained-v0` executor baseline and stays visible in the dashboard instead of being flattened into a fast-route claim.
baseline_card.hull_cache_target_posture: `hull_cache` remains the admitted fast-route target on the executor family, but it stays subordinate to baseline truth and fallback posture outside the admitted workload envelope.
baseline_card.detail: The baseline panel keeps the frozen frequent and promotion pack truth visible beside local run cards so weekly review can compare candidate evidence against the current `trained-v0` floor without leaving the local-cluster dashboard surface.

metrics.detail: The `local_4080_cuda_tailnet_x86_64` panel keeps the retained completed-step count, final loss, and failure fact count from the cumulative ledger row instead of recomputing a second metric view.

throughput.detail: Throughput on `local_4080_cuda_tailnet_x86_64` stays projected directly from the ledger metric posture so the dashboard side-by-side view never drifts from the retained row facts.

current_best_card.detail: The `local_4080_cuda_tailnet_x86_64` dashboard card keeps metrics, throughput, recovery, export status, and budget burn together for weekly local-cluster review.

metrics.detail: The `local_mac_mlx_aarch64` panel keeps the retained completed-step count, final loss, and failure fact count from the cumulative ledger row instead of recomputing a second metric view.

throughput.detail: Throughput on `local_mac_mlx_aarch64` stays projected directly from the ledger metric posture so the dashboard side-by-side view never drifts from the retained row facts.

candidate_card.detail: The `local_mac_mlx_aarch64` dashboard card keeps metrics, throughput, recovery, export status, and budget burn together for weekly local-cluster review.

columns.detail: The current-best column keeps the retained winner visible inside the shared local-cluster review surface without hiding which admitted profile currently owns the stronger row.
The candidate column keeps the retained challenger visible inside the same side-by-side review surface while preserving its explicit export and recovery posture.

profile_comparison.detail: The profile comparison keeps the admitted MLX and 4080 rows side-by-side under one shared device-matrix search key so review can compare throughput, export, recovery, and budget burn without reconstructing cross-packet context by hand.

summary: The admitted executor lane now has one canonical dashboard packet that keeps the frozen baseline, the retained current-best row, and the retained candidate row visible together. Metrics, throughput, recovery, export, and budget burn now project from the same retained ledger instead of separate packet prose.
